name: Slow client

injection.description: A fraction of streaming clients consume the SSE response at a deliberately slow read rate (workload slow_client profile), far below token production rate.

expected_gateway_semantics: Per-stream write buffering stays bounded; a write deadline is enforced per stream.

When the bound/deadline is exceeded, the gateway closes the stream and propagates cancellation upstream (HTTP body close) so the engine aborts generation and releases the request's resources — release is observable (Contract 4 release_observability).
Slow consumers never cause head-of-line blocking or memory growth affecting other streams.
expected_client_visible_behavior: Slow clients see their stream closed after the write deadline; tokens delivered before the close are retained and billable (Contract 1 cancellation billing rule).
Normal-speed clients are unaffected: their TTFT/ITL stay at baseline.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: returns to baseline after the write-deadline closes — engine-side release is confirmed, not assumed
decreases when the gateway aborts the slow streams (canonical key via name_mapping)
slow streams terminate at the write deadline instead of growing unbounded

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: p95 for the normal-speed client population stays within 10% of baseline

abort_condition: Abort the injection if gateway memory grows monotonically with slow-client count (unbounded buffering), or if normal-speed clients' TTFT p95 degrades more than 10% from baseline.
